insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 187, 'Danger Warning: Things to Note When Trekking in the Rocky Mountain Area of ​​Cat Ba.', '

Trekking in the rocky mountains of Cat Ba carries many risks due to the sharp, steep, and extremely slippery limestone (karst) terrain when humidity is high. To ensure safety, visitors must equip themselves with hiking shoes that have specialized grip, use protective gloves to avoid having the cat's-ear rocks cut their hands, and absolutely never split off from the group on their own in the dense forest areas of the National Park.

Why does the rocky terrain of Cat Ba carry so many danger warnings?

Jade Island Cat Ba is famous not only for its poetic beaches but is also a paradise for adventure enthusiasts. However, behind that majestic beauty lie danger warnings that everyone needs to keep in mind. The terrain here is mainly long-weathered limestone, forming cat's-ear rock outcrops as sharp as razor blades.

Clearly understanding the things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba is a prerequisite before you set foot here. Unlike the earthen mountains in the northern highland provinces, Cat Ba's limestone is very hard but easily chipped, creating sharp edges that can tear shoes and clothing and cause deep injuries if you accidentally slip. In particular, the humidity blowing in from the sea combined with the dense forest canopy keeps the rock surfaces constantly damp and moss-covered, creating unpredictable slippery "traps".

Things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba regarding equipment preparation

A safe trip begins with preparing your equipment properly. Never underestimate the danger warnings from those who went before about using flimsy gear.

Hiking shoes - The most important shield

Shoes are the most important item in the list of things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba. You should not use ordinary running shoes or flat-soled canvas shoes.

  • Sole: Must have deep lugs, made of load-bearing rubber (such as Vibram soles) to increase the contact area and grip on smooth limestone.
  • Shoe collar: Choose a high or mid-cut design to protect your ankle from rolling (sprains) when moving over rough terrain.
  • Water resistance: Should have a waterproof membrane while still being breathable so your feet do not feel stuffy in the tropical environment.

Protective gloves and specialized clothing

When moving across rocky mountains, having to use your hands to grip the cliff face is unavoidable. One of the things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba that few people pay attention to is the pair of gloves.

  • Gloves: Choose ones with rubber or suede palms to protect your hands from the razor-sharp rock edges.
  • Clothing: Use quick-drying, highly durable fabric to avoid tearing when rubbing against thorny plants or cat's-ear rocks. Long pants are a mandatory choice to avoid insects and scratches.

 

Danger warnings: How to recognize risks from nature?

Cat Ba's nature is very abundant but also full of risks if you lack experience. Reading and understanding the danger warnings from the surrounding environment will help you make the right decisions.

  • Sudden weather changes: Unexpected downpours can turn a dry path into a deadly "slide" in just a few minutes. If it rains, the most important thing to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba is to find a safe shelter and stop climbing immediately.
  • Wildlife: Cat Ba is home to many species of venomous snakes and insects. Entering dark rock crevices or bushes without observing carefully is reckless.
  • Disorientation: The undulating limestone mountain system looks very similar, making it easy to lose your way without GPS or a local guide.

In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

Things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba to maintain good fitness

Rocky mountain trekking burns twice as much energy as walking on flat ground. To avoid the danger warnings related to exhaustion or heat stroke, you need a sensible strategy for distributing your energy.

Hydration and electrolyte strategy

In Cat Ba's dense forest, humidity is regularly above 80%, causing the body to sweat continuously without the sweat evaporating, leading to rapid dehydration.

  • Drink small sips of water every 15 minutes; do not wait until you are thirsty to drink.
  • Use effervescent electrolyte tablets to replenish the mineral salts lost through sweat. This is an extremely important thing to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba to avoid cramping in the middle of a cliff.

Breathing control technique

When facing steep rock slopes, maintain a steady breathing rhythm in time with your steps. Do not try to climb too fast and run out of breath. If you feel your heart beating excessively fast, stop and rest in a spot with stable footing. Losing control of your breathing is the first danger warning that your body is overloaded.

How to move safely on karst limestone terrain?

Movement technique is a vital factor that helps you properly follow the things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba. Apply the following rules:

  • The three-point contact rule: When climbing, always ensure at least three solid points of contact (two hands and one foot, or two feet and one hand) on the rock.
  • Check the rock's stability: Limestone may look solid but can be crumbly or come loose. Lightly tap it with the tip of your foot or hand before placing your full body weight on it.
  • Lower your center of gravity when descending: When going downhill, slightly bend your knees, keep your center of gravity low, and lean slightly forward to increase the grip of your soles.

Ignoring these techniques often leads to danger warnings about spinal injuries or broken bones from falling from a height.

Danger warnings from the flora and insects in the Cat Ba forest

Besides the terrain, the ecosystem here also requires you to keep certain things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba in mind to protect your skin and health.

  • Thorny plants and toxic sap: Some plants such as lacquer trees or thorny vines can cause allergies or serious dermatitis.
  • Leeches and forest mosquitoes: Use a dedicated insect spray and apply mosquito repellent cream to exposed skin areas.

 

The 5-step process for handling incidents in the rocky mountains of Cat Ba

If you unfortunately encounter situations that fall under the danger warnings, you need to calmly follow these steps:

  • Stop and stabilize your mind: Absolutely do not panic and run around, as this can easily lead to secondary accidents on the sharp rocks.
  • On-site first aid: Use your personal medical kit to stop bleeding or immobilize a wound if you have collided with the rocks.
  • Determine your location: Use an offline map or GPS to know where you are.
  • Send a rescue signal: Use a rescue whistle or phone (if there is signal) to contact the national park management board.
  • Find a safe shelter: Stay away from cliffs at risk of landslides if there is heavy rain.

Remembering these things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba can save your life in the most critical situations.

Why choose a healing trekking tour instead of going solo?

Many young people today prefer going solo to freely explore. However, in the face of the danger warnings about the complex terrain, joining a professional tour, especially a healing trekking tour, offers many benefits:

  • Absolute safety: There are guides who know every slab of rock and tree along the way.
  • A profound experience: You not only climb mountains but also learn how to connect with nature and practice mindfulness in the ancient forest.
  • Equipment support: Reputable operators will provide detailed things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba and supply protective equipment that meets international standards.

Frequently asked questions about safety when trekking in Cat Ba

1. What is the most dangerous time to go rocky-mountain trekking in Cat Ba?

The most dangerous time is during the rainy season (from June to August) and on days with thick fog in winter. Rain makes the limestone extremely slippery, while fog reduces visibility, making it easy to misstep into deep rock crevices. This is the foremost danger warning you need to avoid.

2. Can children take part in trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba?

Children aged 10 and older, with good fitness and close adult supervision, can take part in the short routes. However, parents must strictly follow the things to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba specifically for young children, such as preparing specialized shoes and compact gloves.

3. What should I do if I encounter a snake on the trekking route?

Stand still and slowly back away. Most snakes will not attack if they do not feel threatened. Absolutely do not use a stick to chase them away or deliberately kill them, as this action can provoke them to attack back. This is a thing to note when trekking in the rocky mountain areas of Cat Ba regarding conservation and personal safety.

4. Is there phone signal in the deep rocky mountain areas?

Phone signal in Cat Ba is very intermittent when you go deep into valleys or onto precarious cliffs. Therefore, do not rely too much on your phone. Inform your relatives or hotel of your itinerary before you go. Losing contact is a noteworthy danger warning for solo groups.
